### Post by Chymera on 2007-07-31
I cant unmount my ntfs partition, neither by rightclick>unmount neither through gparted.
In gparted it just dosnt work, rightclick>unmount gives the following error msg: 
> unmount: /media/sda1 mount disagrees with fstab

```
cat /etc/fstab
```

### Post by zidane2 on 2007-08-01
sudo umount /dev/sda1 
should work

---

### Post by Chymera on 2007-08-02
hmmm, 
> sudo umount /dev/sda1 
gives the following output
> umount: /dev/sda1: not mounted

however, > sudo umount /media/sda1 seems to work
but why cant i umnount it through rightclick->unmount or in gparted?

furthermore, to get it back on i cant use 
> sudo mount /media/sda1 i have to use > sudo mount /dev/sda1
I find that quite suspicious since i needed to mention the other location in order to unmount it

---

### Post by Martym on 2008-05-11
This [Linky]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=543293") mentions not using the UUID (like you have for that drive) as a solution to this issue (last page has it)
